slow_queries.query:
SELECT wal->>$5 as type,
       wal->>$6 as schema,
       wal->>$7 as table,
       COALESCE(wal->>$8, $9) as columns,
       COALESCE(wal->>$10, $11) as record,
       COALESCE(wal->>$12, $13) as old_record,
       wal->>$14 as commit_timestamp,
       subscription_ids,
       errors
FROM realtime.list_changes($1, $2, $3, $4)

SELECT wal->>$5 as type,
       wal->>$6 as schema,
       wal->>$7 as table,
       wal->>$8 as columns,
       wal->>$9 as record,
       wal->>$10 as old_record,
       wal->>$11 as commit_timestamp,
       subscription_ids,
       errors
FROM realtime.list_changes($1, $2, $3, $4)

WITH pgrst_source AS ( SELECT "public"."therapists"."id", "public"."therapists"."email", "public"."therapists"."full_name", "public"."therapists"."first_name", "public"."therapists"."middle_name", "public"."therapists"."last_name", "public"."therapists"."title", "public"."therapists"."facility", "public"."therapists"."employee_type", "public"."therapists"."staff_id", "public"."therapists"."supervisor", "public"."therapists"."status", "public"."therapists"."specialties", "public"."therapists"."service_type", "public"."therapists"."npi_number", "public"."therapists"."medicaid_id", "public"."therapists"."practitioner_id", "public"."therapists"."taxonomy_code", "public"."therapists"."time_zone", "public"."therapists"."phone", "public"."therapists"."street", "public"."therapists"."city", "public"."therapists"."state", "public"."therapists"."zip_code", "public"."therapists"."latitude", "public"."therapists"."longitude", "public"."therapists"."service_radius_km", "public"."therapists"."max_daily_travel_minutes", "public"."therapists"."preferred_areas", "public"."therapists"."avoid_rush_hour", "public"."therapists"."rbt_number", "public"."therapists"."bcba_number", "public"."therapists"."weekly_hours_min", "public"."therapists"."weekly_hours_max", "public"."therapists"."availability_hours", "public"."therapists"."max_clients", "public"."therapists"."created_at", "public"."therapists"."deleted_at" FROM "public"."therapists"  ORDER BY "public"."therapists"."full_name" ASC  LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2 )  SELECT $3::bigint AS total_result_set, pg_catalog.count(_postgrest_t) AS page_total, coalesce(json_agg(_postgrest_t), $4) AS body, nullif(current_setting($5, $6), $7) AS response_headers, nullif(current_setting($8, $9), $10) AS response_status, $11 AS response_inserted FROM ( SELECT * FROM pgrst_source ) _postgrest_t

WITH pgrst_source AS ( SELECT "public"."clients"."id", "public"."clients"."full_name", "public"."clients"."email", "public"."clients"."therapist_id", row_to_json("clients_therapist_1".*)::jsonb AS "therapist" FROM "public"."clients" LEFT JOIN LATERAL ( SELECT "therapists_1"."full_name" FROM "public"."therapists" AS "therapists_1" WHERE "therapists_1"."id" = "public"."clients"."therapist_id"   LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2 ) AS "clients_therapist_1" ON $5  ORDER BY "public"."clients"."full_name" ASC  LIMIT $3 OFFSET $4 )  SELECT $6::bigint AS total_result_set, pg_catalog.count(_postgrest_t) AS page_total, coalesce(json_agg(_postgrest_t), $7) AS body, nullif(current_setting($8, $9), $10) AS response_headers, nullif(current_setting($11, $12), $13) AS response_status, $14 AS response_inserted FROM ( SELECT * FROM pgrst_source ) _postgrest_t

WITH pgrst_source AS ( SELECT "public"."client_therapist_links"."client_id", "public"."client_therapist_links"."therapist_id", row_to_json("client_therapist_links_therapist_1".*)::jsonb AS "therapist" FROM "public"."client_therapist_links" LEFT JOIN LATERAL ( SELECT "therapists_1"."full_name" FROM "public"."therapists" AS "therapists_1" WHERE "therapists_1"."id" = "public"."client_therapist_links"."therapist_id"   LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2 ) AS "client_therapist_links_therapist_1" ON $5   LIMIT $3 OFFSET $4 )  SELECT $6::bigint AS total_result_set, pg_catalog.count(_postgrest_t) AS page_total, coalesce(json_agg(_postgrest_t), $7) AS body, nullif(current_setting($8, $9), $10) AS response_headers, nullif(current_setting($11, $12), $13) AS response_status, $14 AS response_inserted FROM ( SELECT * FROM pgrst_source ) _postgrest_t

WITH pgrst_source AS ( SELECT "public"."clients"."id", "public"."clients"."client_id", "public"."clients"."full_name", "public"."clients"."first_name", "public"."clients"."middle_name", "public"."clients"."last_name", "public"."clients"."email", "public"."clients"."gender", "public"."clients"."date_of_birth", "public"."clients"."service_preference", "public"."clients"."availability_hours", "public"."clients"."insurance_info", "public"."clients"."phone", "public"."clients"."cin_number", "public"."clients"."address_line1", "public"."clients"."address_line2", "public"."clients"."city", "public"."clients"."state", "public"."clients"."zip_code", "public"."clients"."parent1_first_name", "public"."clients"."parent1_last_name", "public"."clients"."parent1_phone", "public"."clients"."parent1_email", "public"."clients"."parent1_relationship", "public"."clients"."parent2_first_name", "public"."clients"."parent2_last_name", "public"."clients"."parent2_phone", "public"."clients"."parent2_email", "public"."clients"."parent2_relationship", "public"."clients"."one_to_one_units", "public"."clients"."supervision_units", "public"."clients"."parent_consult_units", "public"."clients"."assessment_units", "public"."clients"."authorized_hours_per_month", "public"."clients"."therapist_id", "public"."clients"."therapist_assigned_at", "public"."clients"."created_at", "public"."clients"."created_by", "public"."clients"."updated_at", "public"."clients"."updated_by", "public"."clients"."deleted_at", "public"."clients"."deleted_by", "public"."clients"."organization_id", "public"."clients"."status" FROM "public"."clients" WHERE  "public"."clients"."organization_id" = $1  ORDER BY "public"."clients"."full_name" ASC  LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3 )  SELECT $4::bigint AS total_result_set, pg_catalog.count(_postgrest_t) AS page_total, coalesce(json_agg(_postgrest_t), $5) AS body, nullif(current_setting($6, $7), $8) AS response_headers, nullif(current_setting($9, $10), $11) AS response_status, $12 AS response_inserted FROM ( SELECT * FROM pgrst_source ) _postgrest_t

WITH pgrst_source AS (SELECT pgrst_call.pgrst_scalar FROM (SELECT "public"."get_dashboard_data"() pgrst_scalar) pgrst_call) SELECT $3::bigint AS total_result_set, $4 AS page_total, coalesce(json_agg(_postgrest_t.pgrst_scalar)->$5, $6) AS body, nullif(current_setting($7, $8), $9) AS response_headers, nullif(current_setting($10, $11), $12) AS response_status, $13 AS response_inserted FROM (SELECT "get_dashboard_data".* FROM "pgrst_source" AS "get_dashboard_data"   LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2) _postgrest_t

with
      tables as (SELECT
  c.oid :: int8 AS id,
  nc.nspname AS schema,
  c.relname AS name,
  c.relrowsecurity AS rls_enabled,
  c.relforcerowsecurity AS rls_forced,
  CASE
    WHEN c.relreplident = $2 THEN $3
    WHEN c.relreplident = $4 THEN $5
    WHEN c.relreplident = $6 THEN $7
    ELSE $8
  END AS replica_identity,
  pg_total_relation_size(format($9, nc.nspname, c.relname)) :: int8 AS bytes,
  pg_size_pretty(
    pg_total_relation_size(format($10, nc.nspname, c.relname))
  ) AS size,
  pg_stat_get_live_tuples(c.oid) AS live_rows_estimate,
  pg_stat_get_dead_tuples(c.oid) AS dead_rows_estimate,
  obj_description(c.oid) AS comment,
  coalesce(pk.primary_keys, $11) as primary_keys,
  coalesce(
    jsonb_agg(relationships) filter (where relationships is not null),
    $12
  ) as relationships
FROM
  pg_namespace nc
  JOIN pg_class c ON nc.oid = c.relnamespace
  left join (
    select
      table_id,
      jsonb_agg(_pk.*) as primary_keys
    from (
      select
        n.nspname as schema,
        c.relname as table_name,
        a.attname as name,
        c.oid :: int8 as table_id
      from
        pg_index i,
        pg_class c,
        pg_attribute a,
        pg_namespace n
      where
        i.indrelid = c.oid
        and c.relnamespace = n.oid
        and a.attrelid = c.oid
        and a.attnum = any (i.indkey)
        and i.indisprimary
    ) as _pk
    group by table_id
  ) as pk
  on pk.table_id = c.oid
  left join (
    select
      c.oid :: int8 as id,
      c.conname as constraint_name,
      nsa.nspname as source_schema,
      csa.relname as source_table_name,
      sa.attname as source_column_name,
      nta.nspname as target_table_schema,
      cta.relname as target_table_name,
      ta.attname as target_column_name
    from
      pg_constraint c
    join (
      pg_attribute sa
      join pg_class csa on sa.attrelid = csa.oid
      join pg_namespace nsa on csa.relnamespace = nsa.oid
    ) on sa.attrelid = c.conrelid and sa.attnum = any (c.conkey)
    join (
      pg_attribute ta
      join pg_class cta on ta.attrelid = cta.oid
      join pg_namespace nta on cta.relnamespace = nta.oid
    ) on ta.attrelid = c.confrelid and ta.attnum = any (c.confkey)
    where
      c.contype = $13
  ) as relationships
  on (relationships.source_schema = nc.nspname and relationships.source_table_name = c.relname)
  or (relationships.target_table_schema = nc.nspname and relationships.target_table_name = c.relname)
WHERE
  c.relkind IN ($14, $15)
  AND NOT pg_is_other_temp_schema(nc.oid)
  AND (
    pg_has_role(c.relowner, $16)
    OR has_table_privilege(
      c.oid,
      $17
    )
    OR has_any_column_privilege(c.oid, $18)
  )
group by
  c.oid,
  c.relname,
  c.relrowsecurity,
  c.relforcerowsecurity,
  c.relreplident,
  nc.nspname,
  pk.primary_keys
),
      columns as (-- Adapted from information_schema.columns

SELECT
  c.oid :: int8 AS table_id,
  nc.nspname AS schema,
  c.relname AS table,
  (c.oid || $19 || a.attnum) AS id,
  a.attnum AS ordinal_position,
  a.attname AS name,
  CASE
    WHEN a.atthasdef THEN pg_get_expr(ad.adbin, ad.adrelid)
    ELSE $20
  END AS default_value,
  CASE
    WHEN t.typtype = $21 THEN CASE
      WHEN bt.typelem <> $22 :: oid
      AND bt.typlen = $23 THEN $24
      WHEN nbt.nspname = $25 THEN format_type(t.typbasetype, $26)
      ELSE $27
    END
    ELSE CASE
      WHEN t.typelem <> $28 :: oid
      AND t.typlen = $29 THEN $30
      WHEN nt.nspname = $31 THEN format_type(a.atttypid, $32)
      ELSE $33
    END
  END AS data_type,
  COALESCE(bt.typname, t.typname) AS format,
  a.attidentity IN ($34, $35) AS is_identity,
  CASE
    a.attidentity
    WHEN $36 THEN $37
    WHEN $38 THEN $39
    ELSE $40
  END AS identity_generation,
  a.attgenerated IN ($41) AS is_generated,
  NOT (
    a.attnotnull
    OR t.typtype = $42 AND t.typnotnull
  ) AS is_nullable,
  (
    c.relkind IN ($43, $44)
    OR c.relkind IN ($45, $46) AND pg_column_is_updatable(c.oid, a.attnum, $47)
  ) AS is_updatable,
  uniques.table_id IS NOT NULL AS is_unique,
  check_constraints.definition AS "check",
  array_to_json(
    array(
      SELECT
        enumlabel
      FROM
        pg_catalog.pg_enum enums
      WHERE
        enums.enumtypid = coalesce(bt.oid, t.oid)
        OR enums.enumtypid = coalesce(bt.typelem, t.typelem)
      ORDER BY
        enums.enumsortorder
    )
  ) AS enums,
  col_description(c.oid, a.attnum) AS comment
FROM
  pg_attribute a
  LEFT JOIN pg_attrdef ad ON a.attrelid = ad.adrelid
  AND a.attnum = ad.adnum
  JOIN (
    pg_class c
    JOIN pg_namespace nc ON c.relnamespace = nc.oid
  ) ON a.attrelid = c.oid
  JOIN (
    pg_type t
    JOIN pg_namespace nt ON t.typnamespace = nt.oid
  ) ON a.atttypid = t.oid
  LEFT JOIN (
    pg_type bt
    JOIN pg_namespace nbt ON bt.typnamespace = nbt.oid
  ) ON t.typtype = $48
  AND t.typbasetype = bt.oid
  LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (table_id, ordinal_position)
      conrelid AS table_id,
      conkey[$49] AS ordinal_position
    FROM pg_catalog.pg_constraint
    WHERE contype = $50 AND cardinality(conkey) = $51
  ) AS uniques ON uniques.table_id = c.oid AND uniques.ordinal_position = a.attnum
  LEFT JOIN (
    -- We only select the first column check
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (table_id, ordinal_position)
      conrelid AS table_id,
      conkey[$52] AS ordinal_position,
      substring(
        pg_get_constraintdef(pg_constraint.oid, $53),
        $54,
        length(pg_get_constraintdef(pg_constraint.oid, $55)) - $56
      ) AS "definition"
    FROM pg_constraint
    WHERE contype = $57 AND cardinality(conkey) = $58
    ORDER BY table_id, ordinal_position, oid asc
  ) AS check_constraints ON check_constraints.table_id = c.oid AND check_constraints.ordinal_position = a.attnum
WHERE
  NOT pg_is_other_temp_schema(nc.oid)
  AND a.attnum > $59
  AND NOT a.attisdropped
  AND (c.relkind IN ($60, $61, $62, $63, $64))
  AND (
    pg_has_role(c.relowner, $65)
    OR has_column_privilege(
      c.oid,
      a.attnum,
      $66
    )
  )
)
    select
      *,
      COALESCE(
  (
    SELECT
      array_agg(row_to_json(columns)) FILTER (WHERE columns.table_id = tables.id)
    FROM
      columns
  ),
  $67
) AS columns
    from tables
where schema in ($1)

recommendations.action: Review query execution plans and add missing indexes
Run VACUUM ANALYZE on tables with high bloat ratio

required_indexes.billing_records_org_status_created_idx: CREATE INDEX billing_records_org_status_created_idx ON public.billing_records USING btree (organization_id, status, created_at DESC)
required_indexes.clients_org_status_active_idx: CREATE INDEX clients_org_status_active_idx ON public.clients USING btree (organization_id, status, full_name) WHERE (deleted_at IS NULL)
required_indexes.session_cpt_entries_org_session_line_idx: CREATE INDEX session_cpt_entries_org_session_line_idx ON public.session_cpt_entries USING btree (organization_id, session_id, line_number)
required_indexes.sessions_org_therapist_start_time_idx: CREATE INDEX sessions_org_therapist_start_time_idx ON public.sessions USING btree (organization_id, therapist_id, start_time)
